title: The Unbelievable Rise of Josh Apiafi
author: Reception
excerpt: On 6th February Hinton Workspace hosted Josh Apiafi and shared insights from his remarkable entrepreneurial journey from jockey to horse-racing business founder.
body:
“I Just Wanted to Go as Fast as Possible Over the Biggest Fences”

On 6th February we hosted Josh Apiafi and learnt about his inspiring story and gained valuable insights from this remarkable entrepreneur.

Josh Apiafi’s life reads like a modern-day fairytale. Leaving school at just 16, he embarked on an unconventional path that would lead him from the racetrack to the boardroom, becoming a successful entrepreneur in the competitive world of horse racing.

“I just wanted to go as fast as possible over the biggest fences,” Apiafi once said, reflecting on his early passion for horse racing. This desire for speed, a competitive spirit and determination to overcome obstacles fuelled both his riding career and his later entrepreneurial ventures.

Building an Empire Against All Odds

Apiafi’s path was not without challenges. As a young Black man navigating the predominantly white world of horse racing, he encountered prejudice. Guided by his father’s advice, “If you want to be the best, why don’t you go to the best,” he secured a position with trainer Martin Pipe.

While his riding career brought success, Apiafi’s entrepreneurial drive led him to explore business opportunities in racing. He promoted products at racecourses, joined Betfair as a marketing manager during its rise from startup to global industry leader, and later founded Rewards4Racing, a loyalty programme that grew to more than 1.2 million members.

title: It Takes a Village to Raise a Business
author: Reception
excerpt: A post about how rural co-working and community support help small businesses grow in Dorset.
body:
It takes a village to raise a child’ goes the old proverb; that speaks volumes about the power of community doesn’t it?

And just as a child thrives in a supportive environment, so too does a business.

So much of village life revolves around connection. A quick chat at the pub, a chance encounter at the post office – these everyday interactions often spark unexpected conversation and opportunities. It’s this very essence of village mentality, that interconnectedness and mutual support, that we’ve bottled at Hinton.

We believe it takes a community to make a business, especially in the unique landscape of rural Dorset. Co-working spaces are built on the principles of connection, collaboration, and the shared spirit of village life, recognising that in today’s world, community is more vital than ever.

Are you toiling away in isolation, missing out on those serendipitous connections? The traditional image of the lone entrepreneur working in silo is increasingly outdated. Entrepreneurs and small business owners, by their very nature, need the exchange of ideas, the support of shared experiences, and a network of connections to flourish.

Co-working spaces mock the traditional office environment, but have gloriously shed the formality and rigid structure for something more organic, more human and more grown up.

Think of it as the modern village square for businesses. Just like those chance encounters at the village shop or pub, Hinton Workspace is a place for those everyday conversations that create opportunities. You might find yourself bouncing ideas off someone during a coffee break, discovering a potential collaborator across the room, or simply gaining inspiration from the diverse mix of people around you.

This collaborative environment can accelerate growth and provide a much-needed support system, particularly for those navigating the challenges of running a small business or freelancing on short term projects with no long lasting connections.

This isn’t just anecdotal either, research backs it up. A 2023 study by Deskmag found that 89% of co-working members reported feeling more connected to their community, 83% felt less lonely and 64% said co-working helped their business grow. These statistics highlight the tangible benefits of being part of a supportive network, a network that mirrors the inherent support structure of village life.

“The greatness of a community is most accurately measured by the compassionate actions of its members.” – Coretta Scott King

This quote speaks to the power of people and sharing, a cornerstone of village communities and co-working spaces alike. We are all constantly learning from others, whether it’s through informal conversations, workshops, or networking events. This exchange of knowledge empowers individuals to discover their own potential and achieve more than they could alone.

We understand the unique challenges and opportunities of rural businesses and have created a space that allows local people and businesses to collaborate, learn, be inspired and grow.

If you’re ready to experience the power of community, the power of the village, in your business life, we invite you to join us at Hinton Workspace.

Discover a new way to work, connect, and grow right here on your doorstep.

We’d love to welcome you to Hinton Workspace for the day… Try us out and see what you think!

title: From Startups to Corporates: Why Businesses Are Making the Move to Flexible Workspaces
author: Reception
excerpt: A post on why UK businesses are shifting from traditional offices to flexible workspaces for cost, scalability and hybrid working.
body:
Ever feel like your office space isn’t quite working for you anymore? You’re not alone. Businesses across the UK are moving away from traditional office setups in favour of more dynamic, flexible workspaces. Why? Because they offer a smarter, more cost-effective way to work, without sacrificing productivity or team culture.

Hinton Workspace brings big-city thinking to a rural setting, offering modern facilities in a professional, relaxed environment. So why are so many businesses making the switch to flexible workspaces?

1. Cost Savings Without Compromise
Renting and maintaining a traditional office can be a financial drain—especially for growing businesses. With a flexible workspace, you get access to high-quality office facilities without the overheads of a long-term lease, utility bills and maintenance costs.
• No long-term contracts or hidden fees
• Pay for the space and services you actually use
• Fully serviced offices with high-speed Wi-Fi, meeting rooms and kitchen facilities included

2. Flexibility to Scale Up or Down
Business needs change—why be tied to a rigid office lease when you can have a space that adapts with you? Whether you’re a freelancer needing a quiet desk for a day, a startup looking to grow, or an established business managing a hybrid team, flexible workspaces provide the agility you need.
• Hot desks, dedicated desks and private offices available
• Short-term and long-term options to suit your needs
• Easy access to meeting rooms and breakout spaces

3. Hybrid Teams Work Better in Shared Spaces
The shift to hybrid working means businesses need spaces that blend in-person collaboration with remote flexibility. Flexible workspaces give teams a professional setting when they need one, without the need for a permanent office.
• Great for team meetings, training days and brainstorming sessions
• Space to connect with colleagues without distractions
• Professional setting for client meetings and networking

title: #10 PRODUCTIVITY HACKS THAT WILL CHANGE YOUR LIFE ????
author: Reception
excerpt: Ten practical productivity tips, from morning routines and decision reduction to focus bursts, goal-setting and networking.
body:
The traditional “grind culture” is outdated. As Stephen Bartlett says, “Work-life balance isn’t about working less; it’s about working smart.” Here are 10 hacks that will change the way you work:

1. Your Morning Routine Sets the Tone
Mel Robbins swears by the “5-Second Rule”—if you have an idea, act on it immediately before doubt creeps in. 5…4…3…2…1.

2. Decision Fatigue is Real — Minimise It
Steve Jobs wore the same outfit every day to avoid wasting mental energy on trivial decisions. Reduce cognitive load by automating small decisions.

3. Eat the Frog
Tackle your most important, most challenging task first thing.

4. Ditch the Inbox Graveyard
Touch an email once. Decide immediately: reply, delegate, delete, or do it.

5. The Power of “No”
Be ruthless and learn to say “no” to anything that doesn’t align with your goals.

6. Strive for 1%
Focus on improving by just 1% every day. The results compound exponentially.

7. The 90-Minute Focus Rule
Research shows that working in 90-minute bursts maximises efficiency.

8. Write Down Your Goals (Seriously!)
People who write down their goals are more likely to achieve them.

9. Do Nothing, Sometimes
Create “thinking time” for quiet reflection and strategic planning. Being busy does not equal productivity.

10. Your Network = Your Net Worth
Co-work for connection and growth. Being surrounded by the right people isn’t just about making friends; it’s about unlocking exponential growth.

‘Embrace the smart hustle’ – Hinton Workspace.

title: National Freelancers Day 2025: Why Hinton is the Ultimate Space for Solo Workers to Thrive
author: Reception
excerpt: A National Freelancers Day post about why independent workers need community, conversation and a workspace that supports better work.
body:
Freelancers, contractors, remote creatives, solo founders — today’s workforce is more independent than ever, but that doesn’t mean we’re meant to work in isolation.

National Freelancers Day (17 Jun 2025) is all about celebrating the solo workers who keep things moving — the grafters, the thinkers, the doers who show up for themselves every single day. But just because you’re flying solo doesn’t mean you don’t need human contact.

Conversation. Encouragement. A bit of kindness in the workday.

And that’s exactly what Hinton Workspace is here to provide.

Hinton is Where the Conversation Begins
Big ideas don’t always happen in silence. At Hinton, the spark comes from sitting beside other brilliant minds. We’re not just a desk and WiFi — we’re a hub for entrepreneurs, creatives, consultants and founders who want to think out loud and hear what’s next. From hallway chats to speaker events, the conversation never stops.

Co-working Beats Going It Alone
Sure, working from your kitchen table can feel convenient. But it also gets lonely — fast. Hinton gives you that simple, underrated joy of being around people. Whether you’re in for the day or here every week, there’s always someone to chat to, grab a coffee with, or help you rethink a tricky brief.

A Space That Lifts You
Freelancers often end up working in places that don’t feel great: loud cafés, spare rooms, or cars parked in lay-bys. Hinton is the antidote — calm, energising and supportive.

Learn, Connect, Grow
You don’t get a team off-site when you’re freelance — but you do get Hinton’s events. Workshops, panels and community meetups are built to bring people together in meaningful ways.

Hinton is Just the Beginning
Need more than a desk? The wider estate offers accommodation, countryside walks and pub lunches, making Hinton a place for inspiration and client days.

Come Celebrate With Us
Come in for a free trial day, book a spot at The Hustle, or just swing by to see what Hinton is about.

Because flying solo doesn’t have to mean being alone.

National Freelance Day is organised by IPSE, the Association of Independent Professionals and the Self-Employed.

title: Hinton Business Expo – North Dorset’s Big Day for Business
author: Reception
featuredImageAlt: Dorset Business Expo networking at Hinton Workspace
excerpt: An announcement post for the Dorset Business Expo, covering sponsors, themed rooms, speakers and the wider aim of building momentum for North Dorset business.
body:
When we first opened Hinton Workspace, the vision was simple. We wanted to create a co-working space in Dorset where ideas and people could come together. A year on, that vision has grown into something much bigger. Hinton has become a thriving North Dorset business community that believes in the power of connection.

That same spirit comes to life this November at the Dorset Business Expo — a one-day celebration of business in Dorset, filled with energy, collaboration and ideas. What began as a way to bring Dorset’s entrepreneurs and local businesses together has gathered incredible momentum. The response has been overwhelming, with exhibitors and guests joining us from across the county.

We’re proud to have ActionCOACH as our headline sponsor, bringing their award-winning professional business coaching to Dorset. Their team helps business owners find focus, direction and growth — the kind of support that transforms potential into performance.

We’re also joined by two Dorset brands supporting themed spaces:
• The Business Growth Room, sponsored by We Are Chain, exploring innovation, marketing and digital growth
• The Wellbeing Room, sponsored by The Strange Apothecary, focusing on balance, resilience and sustainable success

Throughout the day, Hinton Workspace will be alive with exhibitors, speakers and conversation. The event starts with a relaxed networking breakfast, followed by talks, demos and exhibitor showcases across two floors.

At lunchtime, Alice Fox-Pitt will host a Lunch and Learn discussion with Ian Girling, Chief Executive of Dorset Chamber, alongside leaders from business and local government, exploring how Dorset’s business landscape is evolving and why North Dorset matters.

The Business Expo is about more than one day — it’s about building momentum. Dorset has a vibrant, ambitious business scene, and it deserves to be celebrated. For Hinton Workspace, it reflects what the co-working space was built for: to give local businesses a place to grow, connect and thrive.

The event is free to attend, and there are still a few exhibition stands available for businesses wanting to take part.

Join us on Tuesday 12 November and be part of a day where conversation leads to collaboration, and collaboration leads to growth.
